channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: How to protect your pets from bird flu
item.description: Veterinarians across the nation are sounding the alarm about increasing bird flu infections in our feline friends.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: DNC chair candidate calls for greater investment in GOP states
item.description: In the four-point platform that was first seen by The Hill, Skoufis vowed to “dramatically” enhance the party’s Red State Fund if elected chair.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Southerners are cleaning up from tornadoes that swept the region
item.description: NEW CANEY, Texas (AP) — Cleanup was underway Monday after a strong storm system spawned hail, rain, high winds and tornadoes across the southern U.S. over the weekend, killing at least four people.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and LEKAN OYEKANMI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Treasury says Chinese hackers remotely accessed workstations, documents in 'major' cyber incident
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chinese hackers remotely accessed several U.S. Treasury Department workstations and unclassified documents after compromising a third-party software service provider, the agency said Monday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Serbian court convicts the parents of a teenage boy who shot dead 10 people in a school in Belgrade
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — A court in Serbia on Monday convicted the parents of a teenage boy who last year shot dead nine pupils and a school guard and wounded six more people in a school in central Belgrade.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Census Bureau releases 'population clock' projection for New Year's Day
item.description: Based on current estimates, there will also be one birth every 9 seconds and one death every 9.4 seconds in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, ABC 4

item.title: Cats can get sick with bird flu. Here's how to protect them
item.description: The death of an Oregon house cat and a pet food recall are raising questions about the ongoing outbreak of bird flu and how people can protect their pets.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Northern lights could be visible in upper fringes of the US this New Year's Eve
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — There's a chance solar storms may bring northern lights to several northern U.S states just in time for the new year. The sun expelled two bursts of plasma that are hurtling toward Earth and are expected to arrive early this week, according to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Migrant crisis added to record-level US homelessness: HUD
item.description: Sanctuary cities saw homelessness rates jump thanks in part to a surge of migrants being sent from the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: The world population will be 8.09 billion on New Year's Day after a 71 million increase in 2024
item.description: The world population increased by more than 71 million people in 2024 and will be 8.09 billion people on New Year's Day, according to U.S. Census Bureau estimates released Monday. The 0.9% increase in 2024 was a slight slowdown from 2023, when the world population grew by 75 million people.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Northern lights could be visible in upper fringes of the US on New Year's Eve
item.description: There's a chance solar storms may bring northern lights to several northern U.S states just in time for the new year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYPD operating in 'heightened threat environment' in advance of New Year's Eve celebration
item.description: The NYPD continues to operate under a "heightened threat environment" as it prepares for New Year's Eve festivities in Times Square.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas hat shop closes doors as owner fights breast cancer
item.description: Gutierrez-Krapp said running her shop became increasingly difficult as she underwent chemotherapy to treat her breast cancer.
item.dc:creator: Jesus Baltazar, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Carter and Playboy: How ‘the weirdo factor’ rocked ’76
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — Jimmy Carter already had drawn months of media scrutiny as a devout Southern Baptist running for president. Then the 1976 Democratic nominee brought up sex and sin as he explained his religious faith to Playboy magazine. Carter was not misquoted.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elk with chair on head in Ruidoso is able to move, eat
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) -- An elk with a chair on its head is being reported in the Ruidoso area, but authorities say the animal has been evaluated and is doing OK. The Ruidoso Police Department said Saturday evening, Dec.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: What we know about Jimmy Carter funeral plans
item.description: Former President Carter's funeral will be held in Washington early next month as the culmination of roughly a week of remembrances, President Biden announced in a proclamation late Sunday. Carter's state funeral will take place at Washington National Cathedral on Jan. 9, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican officials dismantle migrant encampment in Matamoros
item.description: The founder of an NGO that operates schools for children asylum seekers south of the Mexican border, says on Friday morning she saw a migrant family wandering in Matamoros, luggage in hands, in an area where a migrant encampment recently had been.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: State funeral for Jimmy Carter will be Jan. 9
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter has died at the age of 100. The 39th president of the United States was a Georgia peanut farmer who sought to restore trust in government when he assumed the presidency in 1977 and then built a reputation for tireless work as a humanitarian.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Charles Dolan, HBO and Cablevision founder, dies at 98
item.description: Charles F. Dolan, who founded some of the most prominent U.S. media companies including Home Box Office Inc. and Cablevision Systems Corp., has died at age 98, according to a news report. A statement issued Saturday by his family said Dolan died of natural causes, Newsday reported late Saturday.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN P. D. HANNON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nasty norovirus is back in full force with US cases of the stomach virus surging
item.description: Cases of a wretched stomach bug are surging in parts of the United States this winter, according to government data.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Five defining moments of Jimmy Carter's presidency
item.description: Former President Carter's post-presidential life lasted decades, putting his career in the White House in a deep rearview mirror in many ways. Carter died Sunday, according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Neukam, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter: A brief bio
item.description: — BIRTH NAME: James Earl Carter, Jr. — BORN: Oct. 1, 1924, at the Wise Clinic in Plains, Georgia, the first U.S. president born in a hospital. He would become the first president to live for an entire century.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lakers send D'Angelo Russell to Nets in trade for Dorian Finney-Smith, Shake Milton
item.description: The Los Angeles Lakers have traded guard D'Angelo Russell to the Brooklyn Nets for forward Dorian Finney-Smith and guard Shake Milton. The Lakers also sent forward Maxwell Lewis and three second-round draft picks to Brooklyn on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fight over spending stopgap foreshadows funding challenges in new Congress
item.description: The messy government shutdown fight this month foreshadows some of the challenges House Republicans could face next year, as a major test on funding awaits the incoming GOP “trifecta.” Congress narrowly averted a government shutdown last week — but not without a bit of drama.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, WGN 9

item.title: Photos: Jimmy Carter, from the White House to building houses
item.description: From The White House to building houses, here's a look at former president Jimmy Carter's life in photos.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: These glasses let you 'see' conversations in real time
item.description: When I heard about AI glasses that let you "see" what people are saying, I was skeptical. But after trying them on, I was proven wrong.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, WGN 9

item.title: Oregon men search for Sasquatch, die in Washington forest
item.description: Two men from Portland searching for Sasquatch and missing since early Christmas morning were found dead in the Gifford Pinchot National Forest, likely from exposure and being ill-prepared.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KTLA 5

item.title: Americans targeted by brutal crimes while on vacation in 2024
item.description: Several Americans looking to escape to tropical destinations or jet-set to Europe throughout 2024 were faced with tragedy instead of relaxation while traveling abroad.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 15 hurt in Florida when train hits fire truck that drove onto tracks after another train passed
item.description: Three firefighters and a dozen passengers were injured in Florida on Saturday when a fire truck with its lights flashing drove around rail crossing arms and into the path of a high-speed passenger train after waiting for another train to pass, according to video of the incident and a person briefed
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: VIDEO: Driver fleeing at 120 mph slams into trooper in Florida
item.description: A driver is facing several charges after fleeing Florida Highway Patrol troopers, and slamming into their patrol vehicle at speeds over 120 mph in Tampa
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KRON4

item.title: Big Lots reaches deal to keep hundreds of US stores open
item.description: The discount chain Big Lots, which filed for bankruptcy protection in September, has reached a deal that will keep hundreds of its stores and distribution centers open.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, KTLA 5

item.title: Dodgers' Ohtani and wife will soon be welcoming a baby to the family
item.description: Between a marriage announcement, third MVP award and World Series title, Los Angeles Dodgers superstar Shohei Ohtani had a pretty good 2024. And now, it looks like 2025 may be even better for the Japanese two-way baseball phenom.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: At least 15 people injured after Florida train hits fire truck
item.description: Three firefighters and 12 Brightline passengers were injured Saturday morning following a collision between a Delray Beach Fire Rescue truck and a train.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WGN 9

item.title: LIST: Top 10 wackiest Florida Man (and Woman) headlines of 2024
item.description: Another year has come and gone, but the wacky antics of Florida residents will live on forever through the internet.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, ABC 4

item.title: Bird flu virus in Louisiana patient likely mutated to be more transmissible, CDC says
item.description: The agency, in its latest update released Thursday, said the mutations were not found in samples taken from wild birds, suggesting the virus changed over the course of the patient’s infection.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Peyote sacred to Native Americans threatened by psychedelic renaissance and development
item.description: HEBBRONVILLE, Texas (AP) — In this corner of southern Texas, the plump cacti seem to pop out of arid dust and cracked earth, like magic dumplings.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH and JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stunning photos show lava erupting from Hawaii's Kilauea volcano
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the world's most active volcanoes spewed lava into the air for a second straight day on Tuesday. The eruption of Kilauea volcano on Hawaii's Big Island has stayed within the mountain's summit caldera inside Hawaii Volcanoes National Park. No homes were threatened.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
